subject: Operating System
question_text: Which one is not the main purpose of an operating system.
option_a: write a document file in MS Word
option_b: manage the computer's resources

subject: Operating System
question_text: Which of the following are the advantages of multiprocessor systems.
option_b: memory and CPUs are accessed simultaneously
option_c: multiple processes can be executed simultaneously
option_d: complicated to schedule processes and allocate the resources to processes

subject: Operating System
question_text: What is the purpose of system call in OS.
option_a: It is an interface for a user to use the functions provided by OS
option_b: It is another name of memory
option_c: It is the error when we access an array out of boundary

subject: Operating System
question_text: Which one of the applications come under security.
option_b: One time password

subject: Operating System
question_text: Which of the following activities relate to memory management in OS.
option_a: allocate and deallocate meory to processes
option_b: get device attributes, set device attributes

subject: Operating System
question_text: Which of the following data is saved in the text section of process.
option_b: local variables and return address

subject: Operating System
question_text: Which activity changes the state of process from running to waiting.
option_b: trap

subject: Operating System
question_text: Which of the following component of CPU is connected to I/O component.
option_b: Registers

subject: Operating System
question_text: How you define interrupt in OS.
option_a: interrupt generated by hardware in a system
option_b: interrupt generated by software in a system
option_d: it is process creation in Unix OS

subject: Operating System
question_text: What is Process Control block in OS.
option_a: It saves the status of processes ready to execute on CPU.
option_b: It saves the status of terminated processes
option_c: It saves the status of newly created processes

subject: Operating System
question_text: How you define queue header in OS.
option_a: It saves the PCBs of all the processes
option_b: It saves the pointer to first and last process in ready queue
option_c: It saves the status of partially executed processes
option_d: It saves the status of processes waiting for input/out completion

subject: Operating System
question_text: What is the purpose of fork() system call in Unix.
option_b: It creates new device to print a file on printer
option_c: It creates multiples files to write on disk
option_d: It opens web browser page to open a webpage

subject: Operating System
question_text: What is the purpose of long term scheduler.
option_a: It selects the process from hard disk whenever space is empty in RAM
option_b: It selects the process from RAM and allocates CPU
option_c: It selects the process from printer and allocates CPU.

subject: Operating System
question_text: How you define I/O bound process.
option_a: The process that consume maximum time on I/O
option_b: The process that consume maximum time on CPU
option_c: The process that consume maximum time on ready queue
option_d: The process that consume maximum time in hard disk

subject: Operating System
question_text: What is disadvantage of context-switching.
option_a: The CPU does not do anything while context switch
option_b: It saves a lot of time in process execution
option_c: The processes run at ideal speed

subject: Operating System
question_text: Which of the following statements are TRUE.
option_a: Every process is assigned a unique number for identification
option_b: exec() system call sends a signal to parent process to wait
option_c: wait() system call overwrite the data of the newly created process

subject: Operating System
question_text: How you define CPU-burst.
option_a: the time consumed by process on CPU before going to ready state
option_b: the time taken by CPU to complete a task

subject: Operating System
question_text: When I/O event completes, the process switches from _______ to _____ state.
option_b: Running to waiting

subject: Operating System
question_text: Set of all processes in the system resides in
option_b: Job queue

subject: Operating System
question_text: Medium term scheduler is also called as
option_b: CPU scheduler

subject: Operating System
question_text: Which of the following statements about parent and child processes is/are TRUE.
option_a: Child process is the clone of parent process
option_b: The child waits until parent terminate
option_c: Parent process will execute faster than child process

subject: Operating System
question_text: If we run fork() system call for three times in a program, how many processes will create (children processes only).
option_b: 8 child processes

subject: Operating System
question_text: Which system call is used to terminate the process normally.
option_b: abort()

subject: Operating System
question_text: What is the meaning of non-preemptive scheduling.
option_a: when running process is completed without interruption, it is called non-preemptive
option_b: when running process is interrupted by new process during execution, it is called non-preemptive
option_c: when waiting process is given priority to make it possible to run in time is called non-preemptive

subject: Operating System
question_text: What is the disadvantage of First Come First Serve (FCFS) Algorithm.
option_b: Processes are executed in Last in First Out order.
option_c: Processes having short CPU burst time will be given priority

subject: Operating System
question_text: FCFS algorithm is preemptive. True or False.
option_b: TRUE

subject: Operating System
question_text: In priority scheduling, which process is given priority.
option_a: the processes having high priority number
option_b: the processes having short CPU burst time
option_c: the processes which comes first in queue

subject: Operating System
question_text: What does aging means.
option_a: In Priority scheduling, when the processes wait for long time, their priority number is increased so that allocation of CPU to them is possible
option_b: In FCFS, when the processes wait for long time are given authority to allocate CPU to them selves
option_c: In SJG, when the processes wait for long time are given authority to allocate CPU to them selves

subject: Operating System
question_text: Which scheduling algorithm is considered as better.
option_a: The one having less average waiting time
option_b: The one having higher average waiting time
option_d: The one having lower CPU utilization

subject: Operating System
question_text: What is the definition of turnaround time of a process.
option_a: Total time a process spend in a system
option_b: Waiting time of a process
option_c: Number of processes executed per unit time

subject: Operating System
question_text: Which scheduling algorithm is also called as FCFS with preemption
option_b: Shortest Job First

subject: Operating System
question_text: What do you know about time quantum.
option_a: It is the time slot given to a process to run on CPU
option_b: It is the amount of time a process waits for input/output completion
option_c: It is the amount of time a process waits for CPU for allocation
